text: Welcome to this video concerning total robotic revision of complex gastrointestinal gastric fistula after laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass. A forty-eight-year-old woman who had undergone a laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass three years ago for class III obesity consulted for wide regain and epigastric pain. An upper gastrointestinal endoscopy and gastrointestinal contrast study show a type II gastro-gastric fistula. Six trochars were used, one, twelve mm pore for camera, two, eight mm pore for the robotic arm, two, twelve mm and five mm pore for stapling and assistance, and one additional five mm pore for liver protection.

The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the left lobe of the liver, small gastric pouch, alimentary limb, and gastrojejunostomy using robotic scissors. A block resection of the anastomosis reveals a complex gastrojejunal gastric fistula.

The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the proximal gastric bulge, followed by stapling to resize the gastric pouch. A contiguous barbell suture is applied to close the fistula on the excluded stomach, and an epiplasty is performed to prevent fistula recurrence.

The surgeon staples the proximal alimentary limb using three stapling devices. A new gastrojejunal anastomosis is created by Hansen, employing a single-layer running suture technique with absorbable suture material, which is cut without tying a knot.
Methylene blue stained saline is injected through an orogastric tube to check for leaks, followed by the application of ferritin sealant. No drain is inserted at the end of the procedure.
